macro_summary: Clear reversal of yesterday's whiplash: the PM confirmed US-Iran talks concluded successfully, the US authorised Iranian oil sales, and global oil fell nearly 2% on the de-escalation (Business Recorder/Profit) -- though a same-morning Business Recorder piece flags oil already rebounding while markets await confirmation that Hormuz flows are actually clear. Govt formally ended fuel subsidies for farmers/transporters/motorcyclists now that pump prices have come down. Domestically, the oil industry escalated its unilateral-price-cut complaint into a quantified Rs105bn loss warning for refineries and OMCs. PSX itself gave back some of its peace-rally gains on profit-taking (Dawn/Business Recorder closing-bell coverage).

OGDC.summary: The geopolitical premium that had been propping up crude is unwinding now that talks concluded successfully and Iranian oil sales are US-authorised -- both bearish for the oil price E&P realises. A same-day rebound report (oil gains after the selloff, awaiting Hormuz-flow confirmation) tempers conviction, hence medium not high confidence.
headlines: Oil falls after US-Iran talks conclude in Switzerland
US authorises Iranian oil sales amid talks on final peace deal
Oil gains after selloff, awaits progress on Strait of Hormuz flows

PPL.summary: Same de-escalation-driven oil-price unwind hits PPL's gas/oil realisations as OGDC -- mild negative, medium confidence given the same-day partial rebound report.
headlines: Global oil slips nearly 2% after US-Iran conclude talks in Switzerland
US authorises Iranian oil sales amid talks on final peace deal

PSO.summary: The unilateral price-cut complaint escalated into a quantified Rs105bn industry-wide loss warning for refineries and OMCs -- a concrete, material domestic margin hit that outweighs any cost relief from falling global oil. Govt also formally ending fuel subsidies removes a support mechanism.
headlines: Oil industry protests unilateral fuel price cut, warns of Rs105 billion losses to refineries, OMCs
Fuel subsidy for farmers, transporters and motorcyclists phased out amid lower oil prices

PRL.summary: Named directly in the quantified Rs105bn industry loss warning over the unilateral price cut -- a concrete, material margin hit for a refiner specifically, partially offset (but not outweighed) by falling crude feedstock costs.
headlines: Oil industry protests unilateral fuel price cut, warns of Rs105 billion losses to refineries, OMCs

ATRL.summary: Recurring related-party hospital-subsidiary sale to its own chairman -- still a minor governance optics concern, not escalating; low-confidence mild negative carried forward.
headlines: Attock Refinery to sell its hospital subsidiary to its own chairman

NRL.summary: Same Attock-group related-party hospital-subsidiary sale -- minor governance note for the wider group, low-confidence mild negative.
headlines: Attock Refinery to sell its hospital subsidiary to its own chairman

CPHL.summary: Recurring item: Citi Pharma's REIT spinoff of real estate assets -- still a genuine value-unlocking corporate action, carried forward as mild positive.
headlines: Citi Pharma to spin off real estate assets into a REIT

EFERT.summary: Engro Corp (parent) reaffirmed AA+ rating with stable outlook -- a genuine credit-quality confirmation that flows through to Engro group fertiliser exposure.
headlines: Engro Corp maintains AA+ rating with stable outlook

ENGROH.summary: Engro Corp reaffirmed AA+ rating with stable outlook -- direct positive for the Engro holding entity itself.
headlines: Engro Corp maintains AA+ rating with stable outlook

IMAGE.summary: Same Engro Corp AA+ rating reaffirmation -- relevant to the wider Engro group, lower confidence here since IMAGE's link to the rated entity is less direct than EFERT/ENGROH.
headlines: Engro Corp maintains AA+ rating with stable outlook

LUCK.summary: Lucky Group's chairman publicly urging fairer auto-policy taxes is shareholder-friendly advocacy, but it's a lobbying position, not a confirmed policy change -- mild, low-confidence positive. Broader PSX profit-taking is market-wide, not LUCK-specific.
headlines: Lucky Group Chairman urges fair auto policy taxes

KEL.summary: An op-ed on the cost of flawed SOE privatisations is a generic cautionary backdrop rather than a KEL-specific event -- low-confidence mild negative tilt given KEL's privatisation history.
headlines: SOE reform: the cost of flawed privatizations
